implementation_difficulty_reason: A live EW003 constraint needs a new charged-current semileptonic likelihood, inclusive-scheme handling, exclusive lattice form-factor inputs, and optional b->c/u tau nu WET operator matching. This is not covered by the existing Delta F = 2 SLL/SLR/VLL/VRR/LR1/LR2 machinery.

commands: rg -n "Vcb|V_cb|v_cb|Vub|V_ub|v_ub|CKM|semileptonic|B.?->.?D|B.?->.?pi|B.?to.?D|B.?to.?pi|b.?->.?c|b.?->.?u|R\\(D|R_D|tau.?nu|taunu|charged.?current" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n "b.?->.?s|X_s|K\\*|R_K|R_D|B_s.*mu|pi.*nu|nu.*nubar|epsilon.?prime|eps.?prime|t.?->|Z.?->|h.?->|EDM|dipole|conversion|tau.?->|mu.?->.?3e" quarkConstraints flavorConstraints scanParams neutrinos yukawa qcd warpConfig solvers tests -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n "MODERN_PHENOMENOLOGY_SYSTEM_IDS|DEFAULT_DELTA_F2_INPUTS|evaluate_bd_mixing|evaluate_bs_mixing|evaluate_d0_mixing|check_mu_to_e_gamma" quarkConstraints flavorConstraints scanParams tests warpConfig solvers -g '!*.ipynb'
findings: The repo partially covers EW003 through CKM target/tolerance metadata for |V_cb| and |V_ub|.
No implementation was found for inclusive or exclusive semileptonic B decay rates, HFLAV/FLAG split likelihoods, B->D(*)l nu, B->pi l nu, or b->c/u tau nu charged-current WET operators.

status_history.reason: Initial PKA draft for NIT-1 pre-approved EW003 CKM semileptonic-tension catalog addition.

issues: CHK-1: EW003 pdg_or_equivalent value blocks do not carry a year field, and the 3.0 sigma marginal-consistency claim is recorded only under additional_numerical_context rather than as a pdg_or_equivalent entry with full metadata.
CHK-4: status_history contains WRITER-INITIATED but no WRITER-DONE transition; the most recent pre-CA entry was WRITER-INITIATED, and no EW001/EW002/EW003 WA batch worklog was found.

status_history.reason: Addressed CA CHK-1 and CHK-4 findings for EW003 metadata and WA-v2 completion.
Cycle 2 checker re-check passed CHK-1 through CHK-8.
Opus round 1 per-process sign-off; CA-verified PASS retained; see flavor_catalog/signoff/round_001_index.md.

status_history.notes: Fetched cited PDG/arXiv/collaboration sources; measured value claims match.

sections.process:
This entry covers the inclusive versus exclusive determinations of
\(|V_{cb}|\) and \(|V_{ub}|\) from semileptonic \(b\)-hadron decays.
Although the underlying modes are beauty charged currents, the NIT-1 process
id places the entry in \texttt{top\_higgs\_ew}; it is an electroweak CKM-input
diagnostic with direct ties to \(b\to c\tau\nu\) and \(b\to u\tau\nu\) BSM
fits.
sections.pdg_prose:
The canonical PDG 2024 snapshot is
\texttt{flavor\_catalog/references/EW003/pdg\_2024\_vcb\_vub.txt}.  PDG quotes
\(|V_{cb}|_{\rm incl}=(42.2\pm0.5)\times10^{-3}\) and
\(|V_{cb}|_{\rm excl}=(39.8\pm0.6)\times10^{-3}\), with the scaled average
\((41.1\pm1.2)\times10^{-3}\) to be treated with caution because the two inputs
are only marginally consistent, about \(3.0\sigma\).  For \(|V_{ub}|\), PDG
quotes \((4.13\pm0.12_{\rm exp}{}^{+0.13}_{-0.14}{}_{\rm theo}
\pm0.18_{\Delta{\rm model}})\times10^{-3}\) inclusive and
\((3.70\pm0.10\pm0.12)\times10^{-3}\) exclusive, giving
\((3.82\pm0.20)\times10^{-3}\) after error scaling.  HFLAV's 2023
semileptonic report refines the split: the preferred inclusive
\(|V_{cb}|\) input is \((41.97\pm0.48)\times10^{-3}\), while the combined
exclusive fit gives \((|V_{ub}|,|V_{cb}|)=
(3.43\pm0.12,39.77\pm0.46)\times10^{-3}\).  FLAG 2024 gives lattice-anchored
exclusive values \(|V_{cb}|=39.23(65)\times10^{-3}\) for
\(B\to D^*\ell\nu\) with \(N_f=2+1\), and
\(|V_{ub}|=(3.61\pm0.16)\times10^{-3}\) for \(B\to\pi\ell\nu\).
sections.relevance:
The present scan fits CKM observables as output of anarchic quark masses, so
the inclusive--exclusive split is a diagnostic for how much CKM target choice
can move flavor predictions.  In an RS extension with non-SM charged-current
operators, the same observables also set the normalization for \(b\to c\) and
\(b\to u\) semitauonic Wilson-coefficient fits.
sections.post_2008:
Relative to arXiv:0804.1954, the main change is not a new RS formula but a
new input ecosystem.  Belle, Belle II, BaBar, and LHCb now feed HFLAV averages;
inclusive \(|V_{cb}|\) uses mature kinetic and \(q^2\)-moment heavy-quark
expansion fits; and FLAG 2024 provides full-recoil lattice-plus-experiment
fits for \(B\to D^{(*)}\ell\nu\) and \(B\to\pi\ell\nu\).  The same period also
turned \(R(D^{(*)})\), \(B_c\to\tau\nu\), and \(B\to(\pi,\rho,\omega)\tau\nu\)
into standard charged-current EFT interpretation channels.
sections.validity:
For \(e,\mu\) final states these are CKM extraction inputs, not clean
standalone BSM exclusions.  Inclusive values depend on heavy-quark expansion
schemes and shape-function/model choices, while exclusive values depend on
lattice form factors, parameterization choices, and experimental correlations.
Tau-mode translations are more model dependent because vector, scalar, and
tensor operators can change both rates and templates.
sections.code_coverage_prose:
\textbf{PARTIAL}.  CKM target handling exists, including \(|V_{cb}|\) and
\(|V_{ub}|\) tolerances at \texttt{quarkConstraints/scan.py:42},
\texttt{quarkConstraints/scan.py:46}, \texttt{quarkConstraints/scan.py:47},
\texttt{quarkConstraints/scan.py:52}, and the regression check at
\texttt{tests/test\_quark\_fit.py:575}.  No grep hit implements inclusive or
exclusive semileptonic \(B\) rates, \(B\to D^{(*)}\ell\nu\),
\(B\to\pi\ell\nu\), or \(b\to c/u\,\tau\nu\) WET operators in
\texttt{quarkConstraints/}, \texttt{qcd/}, \texttt{flavorConstraints/},
\texttt{neutrinos/}, \texttt{yukawa/}, \texttt{warpConfig/},
\texttt{solvers/}, \texttt{scanParams/}, or \texttt{tests/}.
sections.implementation_difficulty_prose:
\textbf{HIGH}.  A production constraint would need a new charged-current
semileptonic likelihood, scheme handling for inclusive extractions, lattice
form-factor inputs for exclusive modes, and optional \(b\to c/u\,\tau\nu\)
Wilson operators.  This is outside the existing \(\Delta F=2\) basis.
